location.name: Vel-Drassik
location.weather: freezing fog with blood-ochre frost on the stones

crime.motive: to prevent exposure of his theft of the Nightbloom formula

characters.name: Sura Vel-Mak
characters.description: A formula keeper of the Weavings, mid-fifties, with ink-stained fingers and a sharp, careful face that once commanded considerable authority in these halls. Her body lies face-down near the central dye vats, frost already forming in crystals across the back of her outer robe, her arms extended as though she reached forward when she fell.

characters.name: Orrath Denn
characters.description: The trade factor for Vel-Drassik, late forties, heavyset, with a broad face built for smiling and small grey eyes that do not participate in it. He is seated at his counting board when the investigator finds him, quill in hand, making a show of ordinary industry.

characters.name: Thessa Vel
characters.description: The Elder-Matriarch of Vel-Drassik, well into her seventies, with white hair drawn back severely and a bearing that fills a room before she speaks. She stands at the centre of the Elder Hall when the investigator arrives, hands folded, grief and fury competing behind her eyes.

characters.name: Pira Eddan
characters.description: The outpost's mineral assayer, thirties, precise in her movements and speech, with the slightly hunched posture of someone who has spent years bent over a lens. She is still in her working apron when the investigator enters, a notebook open on the bench beside her.

characters.name: Brek Ossun
characters.description: The night tender responsible for the dye vats, a young man in his early twenties, pale and hollow-eyed with shock. He stands as far from the body as the room permits, unable to look away from it and unable to look at it directly.

characters.name: Linna Vor
characters.description: The Archive Warden, forties, quiet and methodical, with the particular stillness of someone who has spent her career preserving things from harm. She is standing beside the central reading table when the investigator enters, her key ring held in both hands.

characters.name: Davan Reach
characters.description: The harbour master of Vel-Drassik, weathered and deliberate, with the calm manner of a man accustomed to managing other people's panic. He is the first to meet the investigator's boat, a lantern in one hand and a cold-log tucked under his arm.

items.name: Iron Bolt
items.description: A short, unfletched iron bolt of the type used in small pest-control crossbows, roughly a hand-span in length. The tip is dark with dried matter and the shaft is lightly coated in pale dust.
items.location_discovery_text: An [item:Iron Bolt] lies near the victim's outstretched right hand, half-hidden by the hem of her robe.

items.name: Parchment Fragment
items.description: A torn scrap of parchment bearing columns of figures in unfamiliar notation — small x-marks used in place of the Kauvari check-mark — with no name, seal, or heading visible.
items.location_discovery_text: A [item:Parchment Fragment] protrudes from the spine-gap of a formula-case on the secondary shelf.

items.name: Heavy Work Boots
items.description: A pair of dark leather work boots with thick soles packed with pale green-grey dust that shimmers faintly. A worn seam runs along the outer edge of the left ankle, and a dark smear marks the inner edge of the right heel.
items.location_discovery_text: A pair of [item:Heavy Work Boots] sit beside the supply cabinet, toes pointing neatly toward the wall.

locations.name: Harbour Dock
locations.flavour: The fog sits so thick over the water that the mooring posts vanish a yard from shore, and the cold-log's lantern seems to float in grey nothing.

locations.name: Elder Hall
locations.flavour: The hall is warm with hearthlight, but the warmth does not reach the corners, and the ochre-painted walls seem to absorb it before it can spread.

locations.name: Dye-Works
locations.flavour: The great mineral vats stand cold and silent, their surfaces laced with frost, and the air carries the sharp metallic smell of viridian pigment and something darker underneath.

locations.name: Counting House
locations.flavour: Shelves of manifests and trade ledgers line every wall, and the single window looks out onto the frozen headland, where the fog has turned the world to a smear of grey and rust.

locations.name: Archive Chamber
locations.flavour: The floor is coated with a pale green-grey mineral compound that deadens footsteps, and the formula-cases on their shelves line the walls in total, careful silence.

locations.name: Assay Room
locations.flavour: Every surface is covered in labelled mineral samples and the smell of reagents, and the frost on the single window has been scraped away in one spot to give a clear view up toward the Archive Chamber.

checkpoints.label: Time of Death
answer_options: between midnight and two in the morning
between two and four in the morning

checkpoints.label: Motive
answer_options: to prevent exposure of his theft of the Nightbloom formula

clues.text: The bolt is short and unfletched, consistent with the pest-control crossbows used in the outpost's stores. The iron tip carries dried matter, but the shaft has no edge, hook, or any modification that could function as a blade, and the bolt shows no sign of being used as a striking instrument.

[char:Pira Eddan] describes the chest wound as a single clean penetrating puncture, roughly the diameter of a bolt — no tearing consistent with a blade, no fracturing consistent with impact. She examined the throat and found no bruising, no burst vessels. She examined the skull — intact, no fracture.

[char:Pira Eddan] examines the bolt's tip under her lens and says the residue is blood only — no chemical coating, no resin, no powder of any kind. She then turns the shaft and studies the dust on it: pale green-grey, faintly iridescent. She says, carefully, that this compound is viridian crystallite — used exclusively to treat the floor of the [loc:archive_chamber] against damp. No other floor in Vel-Drassik uses it.

The frost on the back of [char:Sura Vel-Mak]'s outer robe is markedly heavier and more crystalline than the frost covering the dye-vat lids immediately beside her, despite identical exposure to the same air. The underside of her clothing shows no trace of moisture from the dye-works floor.

The viridian clay dust on the floor near the central reading table has been violently disturbed across a roughly body-length area. Smear marks run from the centre of the disturbance toward the door, as though something heavy was dragged across the floor. A wide dark stain is visible at the centre of the disruption.

[char:Thessa Vel] says [char:Sura Vel-Mak] came to the [loc:elder_hall] at [time:the tenth bell of night], visibly distressed, and told her the trade manifests did not match the formula yield records. Sura left the hall heading toward the [loc:archive_chamber] shortly after [time:the tenth bell].

[char:Linna Vor] says she passed the Archive door on her way to her quarters at [time:the midnight bell] — she tried the handle from habit — and found it locked. She is certain of the time because she heard the harbour bell finish sounding as she walked. She did not hear it open again that night.

contradicts.answer: between midnight and two in the morning
between two and four in the morning

clues.text: [char:Linna Vor] says she locked the Archive at [time:the ninth bell of night] and did not return until dawn. Her key has not left her person — she shows it, still on the ring at her waist. She says [char:Orrath Denn] holds the only duplicate key; she witnessed the Elder-Matriarch grant it to him three seasons ago, and no other copy was ever made.

A small crossbow hangs unloaded on the wall beside a wooden supply cabinet. The cabinet door stands unlocked; inside, a rack of six bolt-slots has one slot empty. A notice on the interior of the cabinet door reads: 'Factor access only.' [char:Orrath Denn] is the Factor of [loc:counting_house].

On the secondary shelf, one formula-case has been improperly replaced — it stands facing outward, its spine turned inward, unlike every other case on the shelf. A thin scrap of parchment protrudes from within its spine-gap.

The parchment fragment bears columns of figures in an unfamiliar hand — small x-marks used as check-marks in the southern merchant manner, not the Kauvari convention. There is no name, no seal, and no heading; only numerical notation that appears to reference quantities and reduction ratios.

[char:Pira Eddan] recognises the notation style at once — she says this is how merchants from the southern rival trading houses record formula quantities. She adds, quietly, that two days before the death she saw a man using exactly this notation at a table near the [loc:counting_house]; she did not know him, and he left before she could ask.

[char:Orrath Denn] looks at the fragment and says he has never seen it. Then, after a pause, he adds unprompted that the figures appear to concern 'the secondary-stage reduction ratios — the most sensitive part of the Nightbloom process.' He stops himself and says nothing further.

[char:Thessa Vel] says that three days before the death, [char:Sura Vel-Mak] came to her with a specific concern: the manifest figures for the last three shipments did not match the formula yield records, and the archive registry showed no authorised copying sessions in the relevant period. Sura said: 'Someone has been inside the formula cases without entering the log.'

[char:Davan Reach] says a small vessel left the harbour at [time:the eleventh bell of night], carrying a single passenger he did not recognise. The passenger paid the mooring fee in southern coin — a denomination Davan did not immediately identify. The vessel had not returned by dawn.

[char:Thessa Vel] examines the fragment and her expression hardens. She says these figures are drawn from the Nightbloom secondary reduction formula — a process known to no one outside the authorised keepers of the Weavings. She says: 'If this parchment left the Archive, whoever took it intended to give it to someone who could use it.'

[char:Orrath Denn] glances at the boots and says they are his — he recognises the worn seam on the left ankle, a repair done at the harbour cobbler last autumn. He then says, with studied calm, that he must have tracked some dust in when he visited the [loc:archive_chamber] earlier that evening.

Held against the disturbed floor of the [loc:archive_chamber], the dust on the boot soles matches precisely — same pale green-grey compound, same faint iridescence. The boot is sized for a large adult male; the sole-length is well beyond what would fit a woman of small or moderate frame.

On the central bench, a cloth-covered bundle — the victim's outer garments — lies beside an open notebook. A line in [char:Pira Eddan]'s hand reads: 'Dust sample from back of outer robe: viridian crystallite. Compound not present anywhere in Dye-Works floor or walls. Source unknown at time of writing.'

The cold-log posted beside [char:Davan Reach]'s station shows gate-check entries at [time:the second, third, and fourth bells of night], each in the same steady hand. The harbour gate seals at [time:the second bell], after which all persons entering or leaving must pass the Harbour Master's station.

[char:Pira Eddan] says that when she examined the body on the [loc:dye_works] floor at dawn, she found viridian crystallite dust heavily deposited on the back of the outer robe — the same compound she identified in the [loc:archive_chamber] floor treatment. She says she found none of it anywhere on the dye-works floor or walls.

Examined in the [loc:counting_house], the boots sit directly beside the supply cabinet. The dust on the soles is viridian crystallite — the compound exclusive to the [loc:archive_chamber] floor — not the mineral grit of the counting house or the surrounding stones.

[char:Pira Eddan] says she could not sleep and was at her assay room window at approximately [time:the eleventh bell of night]. She saw a figure moving quickly from the direction of the [loc:archive_chamber] toward the [loc:dye_works]. The figure was carrying something large and awkward under both arms. She recognised the outline as [char:Orrath Denn] by his heavyset frame and particular gait.

[char:Pira Eddan] examines the boot soles under her lens and says the dust is viridian crystallite — 'identical to the sample from the victim's clothing, and to the compound from the Archive floor.' She adds: 'Whoever wore these boots carried them through the Archive and moved the body. These are not the boots of someone who merely passed through.'

The boot soles are packed with a pale green-grey dust that shimmers faintly when angled toward the light — viridian crystallite, its crust fresh and sharp-edged, not worn. A dark smear marks the inner edge of the right heel. The boot is sized for a large adult male.

leads.text: [char:Brek Ossun], the night tender, found the body on the [loc:dye_works] floor at first light and has not left the building since. He was working the vats all night and may have seen or heard something.

[char:Thessa Vel] summoned the investigator herself. She says she may have been the last person to speak with [char:Sura Vel-Mak] before she died, and that Sura left the [loc:elder_hall] heading toward the [loc:archive_chamber] shortly before the trouble began.

[char:Pira Eddan], the mineral assayer, was called to examine the body before dawn and has been in the [loc:assay_room] ever since, recording her observations. She has already made notes that she has not yet shared with anyone.

opening_narrative:
The fast-boat from the mainland magistracy arrives at [loc:harbour_dock] in the grey hour before dawn, when the freezing fog has swallowed Vel-Drassik entirely and only the cold-log lantern marks the end of the pier. The investigator steps ashore to find [char:Davan Reach] waiting with the careful expression of a man who has already decided what he will and will not say.

The message that summoned the investigator was brief and absolute: a keeper of the Weavings has been found dead on the floor of the [loc:dye_works], and the Elder-Matriarch [char:Thessa Vel] has declared that she will not permit the body to leave the outpost until the matter is resolved. The Weavings are the lifeblood of Vel-Drassik — their trade formulas worth more than any single cargo — and the Elder-Matriarch's authority in these matters is not questioned.

The outpost clings to the frost-bitten headland above the harbour, its stone buildings dark against the paler dark of the sky. The ochre-stained frost on the steps glows faintly rust-red in the lantern light, and somewhere above, a door is standing open that should not be.
epilogue:
[char:Orrath Denn] had been in contact with a southern trading house for the better part of a year, supplying them with copied fragments of the Nightbloom reduction formula in exchange for a sum that would have made him a wealthy man far from Vel-Drassik. He used his Factor's key to access the [loc:archive_chamber] during off-hours, working slowly enough that no single session left obvious marks.

[char:Sura Vel-Mak] noticed the discrepancy in the yield records three days before her death and brought her concern to [char:Thessa Vel], who did not act quickly enough. On the night of the killing, Sura went to the [loc:archive_chamber] after leaving the [loc:elder_hall] to verify what she suspected. [char:Orrath Denn] was already there. When she confronted him directly and turned to leave, he took the pest-control crossbow from the [loc:counting_house] cabinet, loaded it with a single bolt, and followed her. He shot her once through the chest inside the [loc:archive_chamber]. He then carried her body to the [loc:dye_works] floor, hoping the presence of [char:Brek Ossun] elsewhere in the building would make the location seem incidental rather than chosen.

His undoing was the viridian crystallite dust that coated the Archive floor — it transferred to the bolt, to [char:Sura Vel-Mak]'s clothing, and to his own boots, which he had not thought to remove. [char:Pira Eddan] saw him carrying the body from her window. His own knowledge of the formula's structure, revealed when he could not resist correcting what he claimed he had never read, sealed the rest.
